_comment_keys: API KEYS — never stored here. Set as environment variables before starting:
export MEDIA_API_KEY=<server auth key>   # required — used in X-Api-Key header
export PEXELS_API_KEY=<pexels key>       # required when 'pexels' is in sources
export PIXABAY_API_KEY=<pixabay key>     # required when 'pixabay' is in sources
The server will print ERROR and exit at startup if any required key is missing.
_comment_transport: TRANSPORT MODES
http (default) — media server streams files via GET /files/{path}.
base_url: public URL of this server, e.g. http://192.168.1.10:8200
leave empty to return relative /files/… paths.
file_mount_root: unused in http mode.
file (NFS/shared mount) — files are on a shared filesystem visible to
both the media server and the VC editor / pipeline runner machine.
No HTTP file serving is needed; all tools read directly via file://.
projects_root  : path to the shared directory AS SEEN FROM THIS SERVER.
e.g. /mnt/nfs/projects  or  /srv/shared/projects
file_mount_root: same shared directory AS SEEN FROM THE VC EDITOR machine.
e.g. /mnt/nfs/projects  (identical if same mount point)
/Volumes/nfs/projects  (macOS example)
The server saves downloads to projects_root; the returned file:// URLs
use file_mount_root so the VC editor and resolve_assets.py can open
the files directly without any HTTP round-trip.
This deployment:
NFS server (media server) : 192.168.86.33  — data lives at /data/shared
NFS client (VC editor)    : 192.168.86.40  — mounted at /mnt/shared
projects_root  : /data/shared      (server-side path, files are written here)
file_mount_root: /mnt/shared       (client-side path, file:// URLs use this)
transport      : file
base_url       : (empty)
